LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT COINBASE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DARRELL ARCHER IS DENIED. MR. ARCHER SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT, IN CONVERTING HIS BITCOIN GOLD COINS, COINBASE ACTED WITH MALICE AND/OR OPPRESSION AS THOSE TERMS ARE DEFINED IN THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES STATUTE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

ORDER SUSTAINING IN PART AND OVERRULING IN PART COINBASE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT COINBASE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL SEVEN CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF DARRELL ARCHER IS A) OVERRULED AS THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION, NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF CONTRACT; B) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH, FIFTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY, INTENTIONAL TORT, AND FRAUD; AND C) SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RECKLESS MISCONDUCT. MR. ARCHER ADEQUATELY ALLEGES A CONVERSION CLAIM BASED ON HIS RIGHT TO OWN AND POSSESS THE 350 BITCOIN GOLD COINS AND COINBASE'S INTERFERENCE WITH THAT RIGHT. MR. ARCHER ADEQUATELY ALLEGES A NEGLIGENCE CLAIM BASED ON A DUTY OF CARE OWED TO HIM BY COINBASE WITH REGARD TO THE OWNERSHIP AND POSSESSION OF THE BITCOIN GOLD COINS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
Off Calendar Dec-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Darrell Archer, 707-712-9766 Petitioner in Pro through Court Call.
Elliot Joh, Esq. 415-954-0200 of Squire Patton Boggs for Defendant Coinbase.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues two issues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DARRELL ARCHER's Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Darrell Archer's motion to strike or tax costs is granted in part. $1527.42 of the costs claimed by defendant Coinbase, Inc. are taxed as not adequately supported after a timely challenge by Mr. Archer. Mr. Archer acknowledges that Coinbase incurred $1290 in filing and motion fees and approximately $600 in deposition costs, but challenges the propriety of all other claimed costs. "[I]f the items [in a costs memorandum] are properly objected to, they are put in issue and the burden of proof is on the party claiming them as costs." (Sanford v. Rasnick (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 1121, 1128.) Coinbase's sole support for its claimed costs are the invoices it received from its counsel which do not include any invoices or other documents from any vendor. Attorney invoices are not sufficient to satisfy its client's burden to prove claimed costs.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-10-2018 Continued To Oct-31-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Tania L. rice of the Squire Patton Boggs LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-743-2565
appearing for the Defendant Coinbase, Inc;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - Mr. Archer notified the court that he would not be contesting the tentative ruling. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Coinbase, Inc.'s demurrer to all seven causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Darrell Archer is a) overruled as the first, second and third causes of action for conversion, negligence and breach of contract; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth, fifth, and sixth causes of action for breach of fiduciary duty, intentional tort, and fraud; and c) sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for reckless misconduct. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a conversion claim based on his right to own and possess the 350 Bitcoin Gold coins and Coinbase's interference with that right. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a negligence claim based on a duty of care owed to him by Coinbase with regard to the ownership and possession of the Bitcoin Gold coins. Mr. Archer adequately alleges a breach of contract claim based on Coinbase's breach of an agreement to provide the Bitcoin Gold coins to him. Mr. Archer's breach of fiduciary duty claim fails because he has not alleged facts showing that Coinbase owed him a fiduciary duty. Mr. Archer's intentional tort fails because he has not alleged facts supporting any cognizable tort claim other than conversion. Mr. Archer's fraud claim fails because he has not alleged facts showing that Coinbase made an actionable misrepresentation that he relied on, or made a false promise that he relied on, or concealed any facts that it had a duty to disclose. If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Archer has leave to remedy the defects in his breach of fiduciary duty, intentional tort, and fraud claims. Mr. Archer's reckless misconduct claim fails because there is no such claim and it does not appear reasonably possible that Mr. Archer can allege a claim where the scienter required is recklessness other than what he has already alleged or purports to allege. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Tania L. rice of the Squire Patton Boggs LLP, Tel. No. 1-415-743-2565
appearing for the Defendant Coinbase, Inc;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - Mr. Archer notified the court that he would not be contesting the tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COINBASE INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Coinbase, Inc.'s motion to strike the request for exemplary damages in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Darrell Archer is denied. Mr. Archer sufficiently alleges that, in converting his Bitcoin Gold coins, Coinbase acted with malice and/or oppression as those terms are defined in the punitive damages statute. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
